data.title: YZi Labs makes a strategic follow-on investment in Predict.fun, an on-chain prediction market platform.
data.description: YZi Labs announced on the X platform that it has made a strategic follow-on investment in Predict.fun, an on-chain prediction market platform, following the completion of the second quarter of its EASY Residency incubation program. This round of financing includes investment from Susquehanna Crypto, a globally renowned digital asset exchange, aiming to support the continued development of capital-efficient on-chain prediction markets. YZi Labs stated that its continued investment reflects its strong confidence in the prediction market sector and the growth potential of Predict.fun. Reportedly, industry trading volume approached $64 billion in 2025, a year-on-year increase of approximately four times, and is projected to exceed $325 billion in 2026. Predict.fun, through native DeFi integration, transforms idle collateralized assets into yield-generating assets, creating a liquidity flywheel and thereby improving capital utilization efficiency.

data.title: Tesla Misses Quarterly Sales Expectations Amid Market Challenges
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA.O) reported one of its weakest quarterly sales performances in recent years, failing to meet Wall Street expectations, according to Jin10. The company announced on Thursday that its global deliveries for the first quarter totaled 358,023 vehicles, falling short of analysts' median forecast of 372,160 vehicles, which had been revised downward in recent weeks. This marks the second consecutive quarter Tesla has not met expectations.

Investors have largely overlooked Tesla's sales trends as CEO Elon Musk shifts focus to future business lines such as artificial intelligence, autonomous driving, and robotics. However, the traditional automotive business remains Tesla's primary source of cash. Following the release of the delivery data, Tesla's stock fell 4% in pre-market trading. As of Wednesday's close, the stock had declined 15% this year and was down 22% from its all-time high in December last year.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Drops Below 66,000 USDT with a 3.55% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 02, 2026, 13:12 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has dropped below 66,000 USDT and is now trading at 65,992.460938 USDT, with a narrowed 3.55% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Key Developments in AI Agent Economic Infrastructure
data.description: Recent advancements in AI agent economic infrastructure have been highlighted in a report, focusing on x402, ERC-8004, Virtuals Protocol, and OpenClaw. According to NS3.AI, x402 has facilitated over 100 million transactions, achieving an annualized payment volume of $600 million. ERC-8004 was launched on the Ethereum mainnet on January 29, 2026, marking a significant milestone. Additionally, the Virtuals Protocol has successfully deployed more than 18,000 agents, contributing to an aGDP of $479 million.

data.title: IMF Projects U.S. GDP Growth to Reach 2% in 2025
data.description: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has projected that the United States' GDP growth rate will reach 2% by 2025. According to Jin10, this forecast reflects the IMF's analysis of the U.S. economic trajectory over the coming years. The projection takes into account various economic factors and potential policy changes that could influence growth. The IMF's outlook suggests a steady economic expansion, although it remains subject to global economic conditions and domestic policy decisions. This growth rate is seen as a positive indicator for the U.S. economy, aligning with broader expectations of gradual recovery and stability.

data.title: IMF: U.S. Tariff-Induced Inflation Pressure Expected to Ease
data.description: According to Jin10, the International Monetary Fund (IMF) has projected that the inflationary pressure in the United States, driven by tariffs, is likely to diminish. Additionally, the IMF anticipates that oil prices will decline from their current elevated levels. This outlook suggests a potential easing of economic pressures that have been affecting the U.S. economy.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | International Spot Gold Faces Key Resistance Levels
data.description: International spot gold is currently trading within a low peak range, with a significant gap at 46XX being the primary focus for traders. According to Jin10, further resistance is anticipated at the 47XX level, which could exert pressure on upward movement.

data.title: IMF Projects Government Deficit to Remain at 7-7.5% of GDP
data.description: According to Jin10, the International Monetary Fund (IMF) has projected that the general government deficit is expected to remain within the range of 7% to 7.5% of GDP. Additionally, public debt is anticipated to exceed 140% of GDP by 2031. These projections highlight ongoing fiscal challenges and the potential for increased financial strain on government resources in the coming years.

data.title: BTC OG Insider Whale Agent: Trump's Statements Are Political Rhetoric; He Will Actually Continue to Escalate the Middle East Conflict
data.description: Garrett Jin, an insider and agent for BTC OG, recently published an analysis of Trump's national address: This speech is tantamount to a commitment to hot war, and the escalation of the Middle East conflict is irreversible. Jin points out that Trump's claim that "military action is nearing completion and will end in 2-3 weeks" is a domestic political gesture; while threats to strike Iranian power infrastructure and deploy a third carrier strike group are the real signals of action. Easter may become the point of no return for the escalation of the hot war, and the market is far from pricing this in. He emphasizes that this rebound is a short-term squeeze driven by position sizing, not an improvement in fundamentals; there is no way to exit the war, the Hormuz stalemate remains unresolved, the oil supply gap persists, and the risks to importing countries will continue to be exposed. Market reactions have confirmed this judgment: the 6% surge in oil prices reflects the escalation of the conflict, and the weakening of the dollar is only a short-term sentiment. He bluntly states: once a hot war begins, there is no turning back; the world is undergoing a structural transformation, and most participants have not yet begun pricing in this.

data.title: Polymarket's probability that WTI crude oil will reach $120 in April 2026 has risen to 65%, a 25% increase in the last 24 hours.
data.description: Odaily Seer's monitoring shows that Polymarket's prediction that "WTI crude oil will reach $120 in April 2026" saw a short-term surge, rising to 65%, an increase of 10% in one hour and 25% in 24 hours. The event contract rules state that if the final "highest price" of any 1-minute candlestick chart for the most active month of WTI crude oil futures during April 2026 equals or exceeds the listing price, the market will declare it "yes"; otherwise, it will declare it "no." Previously, the rules for the March WTI crude oil price prediction event contract were: if the official settlement price of the current most active month (near-month) of the CME Group crude oil futures contract on any trading day equals or exceeds the listing price as of the last trading day of March 2026, the market will declare it "yes"; otherwise, it will declare it "no." Odaily Seer continues to monitor the prediction market, seeing changes before pricing.

data.title: WTI crude oil surged 13% intraday, currently trading at $112.01 per barrel.
data.description: According to Gate data, WTI crude oil continued to rise in the short term, surging 13.00% intraday, and is currently trading at $112.01 per barrel.

data.title: Ripio CEO Highlights Tokenization's Value in Liquid Assets
data.description: Ripio founder and CEO Sebastián Serrano emphasized the significant value tokenization can bring when applied to highly liquid assets. According to NS3.AI, Serrano noted that stablecoins, tokenized government bonds, and tokenized equities are more suitable for this model compared to NFTs and highly bespoke real-world assets. He argued that these liquid assets benefit more from tokenization due to their broader market appeal and ease of trading.

data.title: Polymarket Predicts Surge in WTI Crude Oil Prices by April 2026
data.description:
Polymarket has observed a significant increase in the probability that WTI crude oil will reach $120 by April 2026. According to Odaily, the likelihood has risen to 65%, marking a 10% increase in one hour and a 25% rise over 24 hours.

The contract rules for this event specify that if the highest price of any one-minute candlestick for the active month of WTI crude oil futures equals or exceeds the listed price during April 2026, the market will be deemed a 'yes.' Otherwise, it will be considered a 'no.' Previously, the contract rules for the March WTI crude oil price prediction event stated that if the official settlement price of the current active month (near month) of the Chicago Mercantile Exchange crude oil futures contract equals or exceeds the listed price by the last trading day of March 2026, the market will be deemed a 'yes.' Otherwise, it will be considered a 'no.'

data.title: U.S. Labor Market Shows Stability Amid Global Uncertainty
data.description: The U.S. Department of Labor reported on Thursday that new applications for unemployment benefits fell to a seasonally adjusted 202,000 last week, indicating a stable labor market in March with low layoff levels. According to Jin10, initial claims have fluctuated between 201,000 and 230,000 this year, aligning with economists' characterization of a 'low hiring, low layoff' environment. Economists attribute the labor market stagnation to the ongoing uncertainty caused by U.S. President Donald Trump's aggressive import tariff policies. Additionally, the month-long conflict between the U.S. and Israel against Iran has added another layer of uncertainty for businesses. A Reuters survey of economists suggests that non-farm employment may rebound by approximately 60,000 jobs in March, though some warn that this rebound could be temporary due to the conflict-driven surge in global oil prices exceeding 50%. Nancy Vanden Houten, Chief U.S. Economist at Oxford Economics, stated, 'We expect the war to delay the labor market's anticipated moderate improvement this year, as uncertainty, slowing consumer spending, and rising costs lead businesses to pause hiring.'

data.title: Private Credit Firms See Pre-Market Decline as Blue Owl Capital Limits Fund Redemptions
data.description: Shares of private credit firms experienced a decline in pre-market trading. According to Jin10, KKR's stock fell by 4.2%, Carlyle Group saw a decrease of 3.7%, and Apollo Global Management's shares dropped by 4.3%. This downturn comes as Blue Owl Capital announced restrictions on redemptions for two of its funds, impacting investor sentiment in the sector.

data.title: U.S. Jobless Claims Rise Slightly, Exceeding Expectations
data.description: According to Jin10, the number of continuing jobless claims in the United States for the week ending March 21 reached 1.841 million. This figure surpassed the anticipated 1.839 million and marked an increase from the previous week's revised count of 1.816 million. The data indicates a slight uptick in unemployment claims, suggesting potential challenges in the labor market.

data.title: Canada's Trade Deficit Widens to CAD 5.74 Billion in February
data.description: Canada's trade balance for February recorded a deficit of CAD 5.74 billion, significantly higher than the expected CAD 2.25 billion. According to Jin10, the previous month's deficit was revised from CAD 3.65 billion to CAD 4.18 billion, indicating a worsening trade situation.

data.title: U.S. Trade Deficit Narrows in February, Exceeding Expectations
data.description: The United States reported a trade deficit of $57.3 billion for February, surpassing expectations of a $61 billion deficit. According to Jin10, the previous month's deficit was revised from $54.5 billion to $54.7 billion. The data indicates a narrowing of the trade gap, suggesting potential shifts in trade dynamics. Analysts are closely monitoring these figures to assess their impact on economic forecasts and policy decisions.

data.title: ZeroStack Corp. Finalizes $107 Million Financing Deal Anchored by 0G Tokens
data.description: ZeroStack Corp., a Nasdaq-listed company, has announced the finalization of a $107 million financing agreement anchored by 0G tokens. According to Odaily, upon completion of the transaction, ZeroStack will hold approximately 21% of the total supply of 0G tokens. As part of the financing terms, ZeroStack will establish a Texas-based company named Texas Blocker to facilitate the deal. Investors plan to contribute 142,232,948 native 0G tokens, valued at $107 million. Following the completion of the share exchange, Texas Blocker will become a wholly-owned subsidiary of ZeroStack. The share exchange is expected to be finalized around July 5, 2026, contingent upon customary closing conditions and shareholder approval.

data.title: The number of Americans filing for initial jobless claims for the week ending March 28 was 202,000, compared to an expected 212,000.
data.description: U.S. initial jobless claims for the week ending March 28 were 202,000, below the expected 212,000 and the previous week's figure revised from 210,000 to 211,000. The four-week moving average of initial jobless claims for the week ending March 28 was 207,750, with the previous week's figure revised from 210,500 to 210,750. Continuing jobless claims for the week ending March 21 were 1,841,000, below the expected 1,839,000 and the previous week's figure revised from 1,819,000 to 1,816,000. (Jinshi)
